statements.question: State the distinguishing properties of solids, liquids and gases
statements.answer:
Solids: Fixed shape and volume, cannot flow, very dense
Liquids: Takes shape of container, fixed volume, can flow, dense
Gases: Takes shape of container, fills container, can flow, low density

statements.question: Describe the structures of solids, liquids and gases in terms of particle separation, arrangement and motion
statements.answer:
Solids: Particles close (touching), regular pattern, vibrate in fixed positions
Liquids: Particles close, random arrangement, move around each other
Gases: Particles far apart, random arrangement, move quickly in all directions

statements.question: Describe changes of state in terms of melting, boiling, evaporating, freezing and condensing
statements.answer:
Melting: Solid → Liquid (heat absorbed)
Freezing: Liquid → Solid (heat released)
Boiling: Liquid → Gas (heat absorbed)
Evaporation: Liquid → Gas (heat absorbed)
Condensation: Gas → Liquid (heat released)

statements.question: Describe the effects of temperature and pressure on the volume of a gas
statements.answer:
Temperature ↑: Volume ↑ (particles gain kinetic energy, move more)
Pressure ↑: Volume ↓ (particles forced closer together)

statements.question: Describe and explain diffusion in terms of kinetic particle theory
statements.answer:
Definition: Movement of particles from high concentration to low concentration. Particles are in constant random motion, they spread out until evenly distributed.

Examples: Perfume spreading, food coloring in water

statements.question: Describe and explain the effect of relative molecular mass on the rate of diffusion of gases
statements.answer:
Rule: Lighter gases (lower Mr) diffuse faster.
Why? At same temperature, all particles have same kinetic energy (KE=½mv²). Lighter particles move faster, so diffuse faster.
